prompt: Using RFdiffusion with symmetric diffusion, design a de novo trimeric self-assembling scaffold (subunit 80–100 residues) that presents three copies of dengue virus envelope protein domain III (EDIII) in a geometry that maximises bivalent antibody engagement for multivalent vaccine antigen development. The scaffold should tolerate loop insertion for EDIII display from serotypes 1–4. outline a methodology for: (1) RFdiffusion C3-symmetric backbone generation, (2) ProteinMPNN sequence design with EDIII grafting geometry constraints, (3) assessment of thermal stability, assembly stoichiometry, and antibody binding. Discuss cross-reactive immunogenicity across dengue serotypes.
source_citation: Derived from uniprot (2024_05); target: Envelope protein E domain III; organism: Dengue virus serotypes 1-4; catalogue version v1.0

_provenance.target_class: receptor-binding domain of flavivirus envelope protein
